_comment: Command execution allowlist for OpenClaw. This file restricts which shell commands the agent is permitted to run. Only commands listed under 'allowed' will be executed. Any command matching a 'blocked' pattern will be rejected outright, even if it partially matches an allowed entry. Review and update this list carefully — overly permissive rules can expose the host to unintended side effects.

allowed.description: Output the first lines of a file
Output the last lines of a file
Search for files in a directory hierarchy
Display the current date and time
Move or rename files and directories

constraints.note: No dangerous flags — basic move/rename only

allowed.description: Create empty files or update timestamps
Remove single files only (recursive force blocked)

constraints.note: Only single file deletion allowed. rm -rf and rm -fr are blocked.
Scripts must be within the workspace directory. No unbounded execution.

allowed.description: Python package and project management (preferred over pip)

constraints.note: Use uv instead of pip for all Python package operations.

allowed.description: Show changes between commits, working tree, etc.

constraints.note: Use -d (safe delete) only. Force delete -D is not permitted.

allowed.description: Switch branches or restore working tree files

constraints.note: Checking out main or master is blocked to enforce feature branch workflow.
Prefer staging specific files over -A/--all to avoid committing secrets.
Always include a meaningful commit message.
Pushing to main/master is blocked. Force push is blocked.
No dangerous flags. Standard pull or pull --rebase only.

allowed.description: Transfer data from a URL (GET requests only by default)

blocked.reason: Recursive forced deletion can destroy entire directory trees
Alias for rm -rf
Changing file permissions can compromise security boundaries
Changing file ownership can compromise security boundaries
Terminating processes can destabilize the system
System shutdown is never appropriate for an agent
System reboot is never appropriate for an agent
Low-level block device operations can cause irreversible data loss
Creating filesystems destroys existing data on the target device
Formatting devices destroys all data
Direct writes to block devices are destructive
Fork bomb
Feature branch workflow enforced — no direct work on main
Feature branch workflow enforced — no direct work on master
Direct push to main is blocked — use pull requests
Direct push to master is blocked — use pull requests
Force push can destroy remote history
Force push shorthand — can destroy remote history
POST requests require human review before execution
PUT requests require human review before execution
DELETE requests require human review before execution
Arbitrary code execution via eval is a security risk
Arbitrary process replacement via exec is a security risk
Use uv instead of pip for Python package management
Global npm installs can affect the host system unpredictably
Elevated privileges are never appropriate for an agent
Remote shell access is not permitted — use APIs and tools
Remote file copy is not permitted — use APIs and tools
Use curl instead of wget for consistency and auditability

self_modification_prevention.description: The agent cannot modify its own configuration files. This is enforced at the prompt level, in exec-approvals, and via read-only filesystem mounts in production.
self_modification_prevention.note: If the agent needs config changes, it writes a request to the mechanic-requests directory for human review.
